skill.name: kegg-database
skill.description: Access KEGG REST API data for academic bioinformatics workflows, including pathways, genes, compounds, enzymes, diseases, drugs, ID conversion, and drug interaction lookups.
skill.summary: Query KEGG pathways, genes, compounds, and drug data through focused REST API helpers.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI
skill.license: Non-academic use of KEGG requires a commercial license

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the external command, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, path traversal, and IP address alerts are false positives from markdown examples and KEGG biological identifiers. The confirmed risk is legitimate outbound network access to the KEGG REST API from a Python helper, with user-controlled path segments and no parameter validation.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Network Requests to KEGG REST API
medium_findings.description: The helper imports Python HTTP libraries and calls urllib.request.urlopen against the KEGG REST endpoint. This is intended for the skill, but user queries and identifiers are sent to an external academic service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct urllib.request.urlopen calls are present and target the documented KEGG base URL. The behavior is legitimate for this skill but still creates an external data disclosure boundary.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated User-Controlled URL Path Segments
medium_findings.description: Most helper functions concatenate user-supplied database names, entry IDs, options, and organism codes directly into URL paths. The host remains fixed to KEGG, but malformed input can create unintended KEGG requests and should be validated before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly interpolates caller-provided values into URL paths. The fixed base URL lowers impact because this is not arbitrary-host SSRF.

low_findings.title: Network-Calling Demonstration Block
low_findings.description: Running the Python helper as a standalone script executes example KEGG API calls. This is not stealthy or malicious, but users should know that direct execution contacts the external KEGG service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The calls are under a standard __main__ guard and are labeled as examples. They perform network access only when the file is run directly.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The markdown backtick alerts are documentation formatting and Python examples, not Ruby or shell execution. The weak cryptography, reconnaissance, path traversal, and hardcoded IP alerts map to documentation text, ellipses, status codes, or KEGG identifiers rather than dangerous operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found markdown code spans, examples, and biological identifiers at the reported locations. No shell execution, cryptographic implementation, filesystem traversal, or reconnaissance logic was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External HTTP Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can make outbound HTTPS requests to rest.kegg.jp through urllib. This is expected functionality but should be disclosed because user queries leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The base URL and urlopen calls provide direct evidence of external network behavior. The service is the documented KEGG endpoint, not an unknown destination.

dangerous_patterns.title: URL Construction Without Input Validation
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper constructs request paths by interpolating caller arguments. Validation against known KEGG database names, formats, and options would reduce malformed or unintended requests.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Interpolation of caller arguments is visible at each location. The impact is bounded by the fixed KEGG host and the absence of credentials or local file access.

content.user_title: Query KEGG Pathways and Molecular Data
content.value_statement: Researchers often need KEGG data without manually building REST URLs. This skill provides focused helpers and reference guidance for pathway, gene, compound, drug, and identifier workflows.
actual_capabilities: Retrieves KEGG database metadata and release information through the info operation.
Lists pathways, genes, compounds, enzymes, diseases, drugs, and organism-specific entries.
Searches KEGG databases by keyword, chemical formula, exact mass, or molecular weight.
Retrieves KEGG entries in text, sequence, MOL, KCF, image, KGML, or pathway JSON formats.
Converts identifiers between KEGG and NCBI Gene, UniProt, PubChem, or ChEBI where supported.
Finds cross-references and drug-drug interaction records exposed by the KEGG REST API.
limitations: KEGG API access is documented for academic users; commercial use requires separate KEGG licensing.
The helper returns raw response text, so callers must parse and validate results for downstream analysis.
Most KEGG operations allow at most 10 entries per request, and some formats allow only one entry.
The helper does not perform statistical enrichment, visualization, caching, or clinical decision support.

use_cases.title: Map genes to pathways
use_cases.description: Find KEGG pathways linked to a set of organism-specific genes before enrichment or interpretation work.

use_cases.title: Connect compounds to reactions
use_cases.description: Search compounds, link them to reactions, and identify related metabolic pathways for annotation.

use_cases.title: Convert biological identifiers
use_cases.description: Build mappings between KEGG IDs and external resources such as NCBI Gene, UniProt, PubChem, or ChEBI.

prompt_templates.title: List pathways for an organism
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the KEGG database skill to list pathways for human. Summarize the first results and explain the KEGG pathway ID format.

prompt_templates.title: Find a gene and related pathways
prompt_templates.prompt: Use KEGG to search for TP53 in human genes, identify the KEGG gene ID, and list pathways linked to that gene.

prompt_templates.title: Convert IDs for integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use KEGG conversion data to map human KEGG gene identifiers to UniProt IDs. Explain any limits on request size.

prompt_templates.title: Trace a compound through pathways
prompt_templates.prompt: Use KEGG to find glucose, link the compound to reactions, then identify pathways containing one relevant reaction. Return a concise research summary.

output_examples.input: Find KEGG pathways linked to TP53 in human.
output_examples.output: A short report naming the KEGG gene identifier, linked human pathway IDs, pathway names when available, and notes about request limits.

output_examples.input: Convert human KEGG genes to UniProt identifiers.
output_examples.output: A mapping-oriented summary that explains the conversion source, expected tabular structure, and parsing considerations.

output_examples.input: Check whether two KEGG drug IDs have documented interactions.
output_examples.output: A plain-language interaction summary based on KEGG DDI output, with a reminder that it is not clinical advice.

best_practices: Confirm that your KEGG use complies with academic-use and licensing requirements before running requests.
Validate organism codes, database names, entry IDs, and output formats before calling helper functions.
Cache repeated results and batch entries within KEGG limits to reduce unnecessary API traffic.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for commercial KEGG access unless your organization has the required license.
Do not treat KEGG drug interaction output as medical or clinical decision support.
Do not send large unbounded loops of requests to KEGG or ignore HTTP error responses.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps AI tools query KEGG REST API data for pathways, genes, compounds, enzymes, diseases, drugs, and identifier mappings.

faq.question: Does the skill contact external services?
faq.answer: Yes. The Python helper sends HTTPS requests to the KEGG REST API at rest.kegg.jp.

faq.question: Can I use it for commercial work?
faq.answer: KEGG states that API access is for academic users. Commercial use requires separate licensing from KEGG.

faq.question: Does it parse KEGG responses automatically?
faq.answer: No. The helper returns raw response text or data. Callers must parse the output for their workflow.

faq.question: Can it run pathway enrichment statistics?
faq.answer: No. It can gather pathway and gene mapping data, but statistical enrichment must be performed by other tools.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
